title: 韓國聲援香港的「反送中」運動

replies.msg:
(2019年8月8日，韓國首爾) 韓國最大的人權團體聯盟「參與連帶」(People's Solidarity for Participatory Democracy; PSPD)聯同韓國基督教青年會(Korea YMCA)、韓國基督教女青年會(National YWCA of Korea)、韓國民主勞總(KCTU)、佛教人權委員會、天主教人權委員會等92個民間團體今早在韓國首爾光化門廣場舉行記者會，發表聯署聲明，聲援香港的「反送中」運動，並要求香港政府立即停止打壓民主和人權的訴求，保障香港市民和平集會的權利。民間團體代表在記者會期間與獲邀到場發言的在韓港人一同高叫「香港加油」，為香港人打氣。

聯署聲明全文及聯署團體全部名單(英文)：http://www.peoplepower21.org/International/1647811

92 Korean NGOs, including Korea's biggest alliance of human rights groups "People's Solidarity for Participatory Democracy" (PSPD), Korea YMCA, National YWCA of Korea, Korean Confederation of Trade Unions (KCTU), Buddhist Human Rights Committee and Catholic Human Rights Committee, co-signed a joint statement and held a press conference in Gwanghwamun Seoul. They showed their support to the anti-extraditional law movement by the Hong Kong people and demanded the Hong Kong Government to stop suppression of democracy and human rights. They chanted "Hong Kong Gar Yau" together to show their solidarity with the Hong Kong people.

Full statement and co-signatories: http://www.peoplepower21.org/International/1647811

Sources: Canada-Hong Kong Link FB;
http://www.peoplepower21.org/International/1647811?fbclid=IwAR282Pjj31q6iU2wtv--JOy7QqdQ-LJTQk-1w6p4ege_yJCe3pithMFdKpo

(Full Statement in English)
The Hong Kong Government Should Immediately Stop Suppression of Democracy and Human Rights, and Guarantee Freedom of Peaceful Assembly and Association

The umbrellas of Hong Kong opened again.

The protests that began on 31 March 2019 demanding the withdrawal of ‘Fugitive Offenders and Mutual Legal Assistance in Criminal Matters Legislation Amendment Bill(Extradition Bill)’ in Hong Kong are continuing until today. The protests started with 12,000 people but has expanded to more than 1 million as of June, 2019. Though Carrie Lam, the Chief Executive of Hong Kong, announced the tentative suspension of the amendment on 15 June, more people are joining the protest demanding complete withdrawal of the extradition bill and the resignation of the Chief Executive. While the Hong Kong government began a violent crackdown on the protesters, the central government of China has ‘warned’ its armed intervention. Freedom of expression and peaceful assembly of Hong Kong citizens is being seriously violated. We, 92 South Korean NGOs, are expressing our grave concerns on indiscriminate and violent crackdown on peaceful protesters in Hong Kong and express our solidarity to Hong Kong people's persistent resistance for democracy and human rights.

The extradition amendment bill, of which the Hong Kong citizens are calling for the complete withdrawal, enables the extradition of criminals to China, Taiwan and Macau, which have not signed extradition agreement with Hong Kong. While the current extradition bill makes it mandatory to go through the deliberation of the Legislative Council of Hong Kong to extradite criminals, the amendment bill allows the court to make decisions just through a review of documents without interrogation process if the Chief Executive decides to do so. Under the situation that the involvement and pressure of China are increasing, Hong Kong people are concerned that the bill, if passed, will threaten the safety of human rights activists, journalists and NGO activists in Hong Kong and jeopardize Hong Kong's autonomy. Given that there are not enough safeguards to protect the human rights of Hong Kong citizens, and that the Chief Executive of Hong Kong is elected by a pro-government electoral college and receive a final appointment from the Chinese government, the Hong Kong citizens' call that the extradition amendment bill should be completely withdrawn is fair. Also, Hong Kong citizens' protests are an expression of their strong desire for democracy and human rights. It reflects social and economic deprivation of Hong Kong people and the reality that Hong Kong's autonomy is not guaranteed.

However, the Hong Kong government is pushing ahead with a harsh crackdown and indiscriminate arrests, labeling the protests as illegal. Police fired rubber bullets and "straight tear gas" at the protesters, leaving dozens of injured and arresting about 500 people so far since last June. The government even indicted 44 participants of the protest which was held on 28 July, on a charge of rioting. The excessive response of the police is a clear violation of , freedom of peaceful assembly and association as specified in ‘the Basic Law’, which is the constitution of Hong Kong, as well as the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights. In addition, on 29 July, the central government of China also labeled Hong Kong protests as ‘violent’ and ‘illegal’, strongly criticizing the protesters for "any act that harms the sovereignty and security of the country is unacceptable," and even forewarned the possibility of deploying the People's Liberation Army. The actual armed intervention of China would result in unpredictable incidents, and it could be recorded as another history of oppression on democracy. It should never happen.

Despite the harsh crackdown of the Hong Kong government and the ‘warning’ of China, the resistance of Hong Kong citizens is spreading to all levels of society. On 5 August, half a million people participated in a general strike, and simultaneous protests continued late into the night. We fully support the desire of Hong Kong citizens to protect democracy and human rights, and stress that freedom of expression and freedom of peaceful assembly and association should be guaranteed under any circumstances. In addition, we urge the Hong Kong government to stop the indiscriminate arrests of protesters and withdraw the indictment. We stand in solidarity with Hong Kong citizens' peaceful and persistent resistance for democracy and human rights.

8 August 2019

The statement is endorsed by 92 South Korean NGOs:

但基本上成個東亞黎講，韓國人係最崇香港
同埋比例上最知道香港同中國係唔同
佢地中年人聽香港歌睇香港電影大
後生都係覺得香港有魅力，仲要一車又一車人黎讀書

佢地支持係唔出奇
多謝支援
俾啲黑粉黎都ok 佢地啲惡評攻擊力好高

韓國同香港無太多利益關係
韓國支持香港真係出自真心支持

相反, 台灣同香港有利益瓜葛
我真係唔信蔡英文係真心支持香港
佢所做既野只係「選舉操作」
台灣選完總統就睇到真面目

咁講實無人聽得入耳, 又話分化撚, 乜乜物物
有啲感動，原來仲有人理香港人

replies.msg: 非常時期，非常手段，沒有永遠的朋友！中共同美國都曾經稱兄道弟。
thank you

記唔記得當日點解咁多人支持我地
因為有做向廢中廢老嘅宣傳
今日點解冇曬？

廢中廢老愛國嘛
我地就要做D大量文宣岩佢地口味嘅
就係令佢地覺得
所有抗爭者都係為國家著想嘅
政府警察先係衰個個
￼￼￼￼


點解後生要抗爭？

因為大陸太多冤案
太多貪官太多勾結
太多慘案太多假野
如果香港淪陷埋
就冇人幫國家守護法治
建議文宣：
最近瑞士銀行 （可以話點解大陸唔查而係叫華春迎出尼嘈，因為啲錢全部都係貪官嘅）
冤案慘案假野揾D新聞有報嘅，咩假針呀個D

￼￼￼￼

大陸衰啫點解要反香港政府？

因為而家個政府同大陸同流合污
成日推D政策係冇用嘅
好嘅又cut
例如比大陸公司用幾千萬整D單車架
比好多錢去大灣區
但又cut長者福利
但又唔扶貧
唔加醫療福利唔加教育福利
但又唔加多D

￼￼￼￼￼

點解壽終正寢唔得？

因為香港政府為左討好大陸
想將任何有錢嘅人送上大陸
先推送中條例
壽終正寢唔係法律用語
要保障自己只有用法定用語
￼￼￼

點解要雙普選？

因為政府只有係民選嘅
先會顧及市民
我地見到尼個政府只會益大陸

￼￼￼￼

點解要講「光復香港，時代革命」？
用毛姨個解釋

點解要提港獨，侮辱國旗？

普通嘅抗爭，大陸人同政府係唔會理
一用尼D口號同行動就全民關注
口號係用尼引起關注，再令大陸人知道事實
行動係用尼表達對政府咁樣貪污腐敗嘅不滿
￼￼￼￼

警察同大陸無關

唔係，未來一哥係大陸受訓
而家嘅警察已經上左大陸電視
佢地已經學左大陸嘅衰野
公開指責上司
佢地已經學大陸貪官收錢
班建制將應該用係廢中廢老身上嘅錢
捐曬比警察
￼

點解示威者要暴力？

大陸政府成日鎮壓大陸人民
文宣要用同胞尼個terms
而大陸政府發現香港人支持人民
所以派人打市民
示威者要反抗保護市民
同向大陸政府講我地唔怕
我地會支持同胞

￼￼
點解要獨立調查？

因為要證明政府警察同大陸政府嘅勾結

點解要釋放示威者？

因為佢地保衛香港國家而戰
為十四億人而戰
何罪之有

反對派唔愛國愛港

泛民，民陣，示威者都係愛國愛港
佢地唔會同大陸貪官勾結
唔會向大陸輸送利益
有幫被欺壓嘅大陸人發聲

反對派顛覆國家安全

反對派冇兵冇槍
反而係警察作反
公開叫張建宗辭職
￼￼￼￼￼￼

為左令廢老廢中支持我地，我宣佈保家衛國文宣行動正式開始
https://lih.kg/1430838
- 分享自 LIHKG 討論區

自推左一日啦
